page: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a General Purpose virtual machine within the Cloud Compute family. It features 24 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports key instruction set extensions, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX and AVX512F), AES cryptography, and Bit Manipulation Instructions (BMI2). However, the CPU allocation is shared, which introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs compared to dedicated-core instances. This shared architecture is reflected in the stress-ng benchmark results, where the server achieved a single-core CPU performance score of 1451.3 and a multi-core CPU performance score of 32078.94, both placing within the average (middle 50%) tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers high resource density, combining a large 1.6 TB NVMe storage allocation with substantial memory and core counts on a shared-resource model. This makes it a viable option for workloads that require significant storage capacity and memory but do not demand sustained, non-blocking CPU performance. Recommended workloads include general-purpose web serving, development environments, staging databases, and asynchronous task processing.
description: The Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a General Purpose virtual server from the Cloud Compute family, configured with 24 vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, this instance features a shared CPU allocation model. It comes bundled with 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrated average performance, scoring 1451.3 in stress-ng single-core and 32078.94 in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests. While the shared CPU architecture presents potential resource contention tradeoffs, the high storage and memory density provide qualitative cost efficiency for resource-heavy workloads. It is suitable for general-purpose applications, staging databases, development environments, and web serving.
og_description: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a General Purpose server featuring 24 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for database staging and web serving.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual server offering high-capacity NVMe storage and substantial memory on a shared CPU architecture.
